title: EIP-7961: EVM64 - EOF code section
description: Defines a "pure" EVM64 mode as a separate EOF code section type. All opcodes in EVM64 mode operate only on the least significant 64 bits in little endian. This is an alternative to EIP-7937's prefix-opcode approach, trading system call interoperability for shorter code size by eliminating multibyte opcodes. Requires EIP-3540, EIP-4750, and EIP-7960.
author: Wei Tang (@sorpaas)
laymanDescription: Adds a new 64-bit execution mode inside EOF smart contracts. Instead of using the standard 256-bit math the EVM normally uses, contracts can opt into a faster 64-bit mode for code sections that do not need large numbers, potentially improving execution speed and reducing code size.

endUsers.description: No direct impact. If adopted, contracts using EVM64 sections could execute more efficiently, but user-facing behavior is unchanged.

appDevs.description: Developers targeting EOF contracts gain an optional 64-bit execution mode with reduced code size, but lose access to system call opcodes within those sections.

walletDevs.description: No impact. Wallet transaction construction and signing are unaffected by internal EVM execution mode changes.

toolingInfra.description: Compilers, debuggers, and disassemblers must recognize the new EOF code section type and support 64-bit opcode semantics with little-endian encoding.

layer2s.description: Layer 2 fraud proofs and EVM-equivalent execution environments would need to support the new EVM64 code section type if deployed.

elClients.description: Execution clients must implement a new EOF code section type, 64-bit opcode variants, adjusted gas schedules, and EOF validation changes.

clClients.description: No direct impact. EVM64 is purely an execution-layer change with no consensus-layer protocol modifications.

benefits: Produces shorter contract bytecode by eliminating the need for multibyte prefix opcodes.
Enables EVM interpreters to optimize execution using native 64-bit arithmetic internally.
Reduces gas costs for arithmetic and memory operations within 64-bit code sections.
Cleanly separates 64-bit execution via EOF typing rather than inline mode switching.
tradeoffs: Only works with EOF contracts; legacy EVM contracts cannot use EVM64 mode.
System calls for block data, balances, and storage are unavailable inside EVM64 sections.
Adds interpreter complexity with a second execution mode and associated validation rules.

scaleL1.description: Native 64-bit execution mode enables more efficient EVM interpretation and lower gas costs for computation-heavy contracts.
